get.description: Get information about the business for which we started a KYC of a beneficial owner

post.description: Gets or creates the business Onboarding, optionally accepting an existing business. You can select an existing business from the results of `GET /hosted/businesses`.

get.description: Get information about the beneficial owners of this business

patch.description: Applies multiple operations in batch to update or create business owners. NOTE: the open API documentation for the request is extremely incorrect for this API.

parameters.name: x-fp-is-bootstrap
parameters.description: Provide `true` if the data in the request is bootstrap data.

patch.description: Updates data in a business vault. Can be used to update `business.` data

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the business's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided vault data is valid before adding it to the business vault

parameters.name: x-fp-is-bootstrap
parameters.description: Provide `true` if the data in the request is bootstrap data.

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the business's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

get.description: Get information about the businesses owned by the authenticated user
Checks a hosted session token for expiration/validity

post.description: Create a new document for the outstanding document request
Process the document and run any vendor verifications.
Upload an image for the given side to the provided document.
Tries to identify an existing user by either phone number or email. If the user is found, returns available challenge kinds.

parameters.name: x-sandbox-id
parameters.description: When provided, creates a sandbox user with the provided sandbox ID. Sandbox IDs allow you to create multiple users with the same contact info. In order to log in using an existing sandbox user, you can provide its Sandbox ID in the Footprint flow.

post.description: Respond to a KBA challenge to prove knowledge of existing data in the vault.

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the user's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

post.description: Tries to identify an existing user by either phone number or email and returns whether a user exists. This is used exclusively in our SDK to check proactively if bootstrapped data identifies an existing user. This is very similar to the normal identify API, but it has a much simpler API will hopefully be more stable than the normal identify API since changes will break old SDK versions.
Sends a challenge to the phone number and returns an HTTP 200. When the challenge is completed through the identify/verify endpoint, the client can get or create the user with this phone number.
Sends a challenge to a phone number or email and returns an HTTP 200. When the challenge is completed through the identify/verify endpoint, the client can begin onboarding the user.

parameters.name: x-sandbox-id
parameters.description: When provided, creates a sandbox user with the provided sandbox ID. Sandbox IDs allow you to create multiple users with the same contact info. In order to log in using an existing sandbox user, you can provide its Sandbox ID in the Footprint flow.

parameters.name: x-fp-is-components-sdk
parameters.description: When a non-empty value is provided, indicates that the request is originating from the components SDK

post.description: Generate a validation token after the user finishes the identify flow.

Verifies the response to either an SMS or biometric challenge. When the challenge response is verified, we will return an auth token for the user. If no user exists (which may only happen after a phone challenge), we will create a new user with the provided phone number
Gets or creates the Onboarding for this (user, ob_config) pair.
Mark the onboarding as authorized and initiate IDV checks

get.description: Get the details of an onboarding configuration.

post.description: Generates a new d2p session token for the currently authenticated user. The d2p session token has a limited scope, and also includes some status metadata for syncing state across the phone and desktop.
Send an SMS with a link to the phone onboarding page.

get.description: Gets the status of the provided d2p session. Requires the d2p session token as the auth header.

post.description: Update the status of the provided d2p session. Only allows updating to certain statuses.
Records a fingerprint visitorID for the Fingerprint SDK in the frontend
Continue processing the onboarding after user this stage of user input has finished
Records a deviceSessionId from the Socure Device SDK in the frontend

get.description: Fetch information from an existing onboarding session token.

post.description: Tells us that a passkey registration was skipped

get.description: Returns the remaining onboarding requirements to complete the onboarding.

post.description: Performs a lookup with Stytch on the passed up telemetry_id and records the response + risk signals
Log a timeline event for this user using a frontend-provided page identifier
Finish onboarding the user. Returns the validation token that can be exchanged for a permanent Footprint user token.
Parses and accepts a user's onboarding device attestation
Generate a device attestation challenge

get.description: Returns information about the auth methods this user has registered.
Returns the set of auth requirements to satisfy the auth portion of the playbook in the provided session
Returns a list of organizations onto which the user has onboarded

post.description: Sends a challenge of the requested kind
Verify the previously sent challenge and attach the new form of contact info to the vault
Records a user's consent to collection of their images.
Create a new document for the outstanding document request
Process the document and run any vendor verifications.
Upload an image for the given side to the provided document.
Used to asynchronously verify a user's email address. Requires the token sent to the users email.

get.description: Returns information about the auth token. Can only be used in sandbox mode for demo tenants.
Returns information about a given auth token.

post.description: Create a new token with the requested scopes using the permissions granted by the existing token. Used to create a secondary token with more limited scope. The new token's expiry time is the same as the old token's
POSTs a document to the vault

patch.description: Updates data in a user vault

parameters.name: x-fp-is-bootstrap
parameters.description: Provide `true` if the data in the request is bootstrap data.

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the user's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

post.description: Decrypts the specified list of fields from the provided vault.
Checks if provided vault data is valid before adding it to the vault

requestBody.description: Key-value map of data to add to the user's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

get.description: Fetch information from an existing SDK args session.

post.description: Create a new session containing args for the SDK.
Log contents of the HTTP body.

Business Owner Token.name: X-Kyb-Bo-Token
Business Owner Token.description: Token to initialize KYC of a business owner. Uniquely identifies a business and beneficial owner.

Integration Testing User Onboarding Toke.name: X-Fp-Authorization
Integration Testing User Onboarding Toke.description: Short-lived auth token for an integration testing user during bifrost.

Onboarding Config Publishable Key.name: X-Onboarding-Config-Key
Onboarding Config Publishable Key.description: Long-lived, publishable key representing an onboarding configuration. You can create and view your credentials in the dashboard.

SDK Args Token.name: X-Fp-Sdk-Args-Token
SDK Args Token.description: Short-lived token representing arguments for our SDK.

User Onboarding Token.name: X-Fp-Authorization
User Onboarding Token.description: Short-lived auth token for a user during bifrost. Issued by identify and contains scopes to perform specific user actions.

User Token.name: X-Fp-Authorization
User Token.description: Short-lived auth token for a user. Issued by identify and contains scopes to perform specific user actions.

can_update.description: True if the user's auth token doesn't restrict updating this auth method

CollectedDataOption.description:
Represent the options of allowed CollectedData.
 Some CollectedData variants only have a single allowable CollectedDataOption.
 Other CollectedData variants may have multiple Options of assortments of data that can be
 collected. Each CollectedDataOption maps to a list of IdentityDataKinds that are represented by
 the CDO

expires_at.description: The time at which the token expires

token.description: The short-lived token that gives temporary access to perform operations for this user

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the document that will be displayed to the user

name.description: The human-readable name of the document to display to the user

state.description: state token to send back along with attestation

DocumentResponse.description:
Response for a identity document request. Errors are non-optional if the identity vendor.
 Requires additional images be collected.

data.description: The token data in the email link fragment

FilterFunction.description: Represents a data transform to apply to underlying plaintext behind a data identifier. Specify only one of the following fields. See more here: https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/proxy#filter-functions

android_package_name.description: required if running footprint sdk in android

ios_bundle_id.description: required if running footprint sdk in ios

HandoffMetadata.description:
Embeds extra information in the d2p token to pass from the desktop to handoff session.
 NOTE: changes to this struct should be backwards-compatible since we may use this struct
 to deserialize old versions

has_linked_user.description:
True if a user has already started onboarding as this beneficial owner. In this case, the
 data below comes directly from that user's vault.

is_authed_user.description: True if this beneficial owner represents the currently authed user.

is_mutable.description: True if this beneficial owner is editable by the currently authed user.

HostedWorkflowRequest.description:
Context on what information is specifically requested from the user, if any.
 NOTE: This is not actually a property of the ob configuration.

IdDocKind.description:
This is the set of document kinds that are verifiable with incode. They are govt-issued docs,
 and they all support having a selfie uploaded alongside them.

IdentifiedUser.description: All of the context on the identified user, if found

can_initiate_signup_challenge.description:
When true, allowed to create a new user via a signup challenge even when there's already an
 existing user with this contact info. Generally, a user can make a new vault IF they're not
 in a context logging into a tenant that they've already onboarded onto

has_syncable_passkey.description:
Signals that one or more biometric credentials support syncing and may be available to use
 on desktop/other devices

matching_fps.description: The list of DataIdentifiers whose fingerprints matched on the vault

scrubbed_email.description: Populated only when identifying a user via auth token that was created by the tenant

token_scopes.description: The scopes of the returned token

IdentifyId.description: An identifier for the identify flow that will uniquely identify a user

Inviter.description: The primary business owner that invited you to fill out a KYC form

Iso3166TwoDigitCountryCode.description:
list of valid iso3166-alpha-2 country codes, from https://datahub.io/core/country-codes#data
 eventually we should maybe just pony up and pay for the subscription to iso: https://www.iso.org/publication/PUB500001.html
 Channel islands does not have a country code

sdk_kind.description: Really SdkArgsKind, but prefer for this telemetry API to be unopinionated on validation

ModernRawUserDataRequest.description: Key-value map of data to add to the user's vault. For more documentation on available keys, see [here](https://docs.onefootprint.com/articles/vault/fields).

inherit_business_id.description:
Can only be provided if there isn't already a business associated with this session.
 The identifier of an existing scoped business, if we want to inherit an existing business.
 If null and there isn't already a business associated with this session, creates a new
 business.

allowed_origins.description: allow list of origins permitted to host the embedded flow

required_auth_methods.description:
When non-null, the provided auth methods are required to be verified by the playbook. Null
 does not mean that no auth is required - it just means the playbook doesn't care which
 method is used.

SdkArgs.description:
BE VERY CAREFUL CHANGING THESE.
 Old versions of Footprint.js may be sending old versions of this struct.
 When making a breaking change to the struct, you should add a new version of the struct to this
 enum variant.

token.description: Auth token to pass to the verify call

email.description: If the challenge kind is email, the email address to send the challenge to

phone_number.description: If the challenge kind is SMS, the phone number to send the challenge to

biometric_challenge_json.description: If the challenge kind is biometric, the challenge JSON for the browser

challenge_token.description: Information saved client side and sent back with the challenge response

time_before_retry_s.description: The timeout until you're allowed to initiate another challenge

challenge_response.description: The response to the challenge. Either SMS/email PIN code or passkey response

challenge_token.description: The token given from initiating the challenge

auth_token.description: The new token issued after completing the challenge

fields.description:
List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`,
 `custom.bank_account`

reason.description: Reason for the data decryption. This will be logged

transforms.description:
A list of filter and transform functions to apply to each decrypted datum.
 Omit or leave empty to apply no transforms.
 Can find more information on allowed transform functions on our docs

version_at.description:
When provided, decrypts the user's data as it existed at the provided timestamp.
 Provided as an ISO 8601 timestamp string, like `2024-01-01T12:00:00Z`.

is_components_sdk.description: The components SDK wraps the verify SDK with the same args

data.description:
Allow onboarding onto the specific playbook.
 This allows editing data, re-verifies data, and then re-triggers decision engine

reuse_existing_bo_kyc.description:
When true, reuses existing BOs' KYC results on the same playbook.
 When false, requires the existing BOs to re-complete KYC.
 Can only be true for KYB playbooks

kind.description:
Allow onboarding onto the specific playbook.
 This allows editing data, re-verifies data, and then re-triggers decision engine

data.description: Upload a new document and re-run the decision engine

kind.description: Upload a new document and re-run the decision engine
